role: ArchSpine is a semantic mirror system that provides a unified, AI-augmented framework for indexing, analyzing, enforcing architectural rules, and synchronizing codebases across distributed nodes. It bridges human-readable documentation and machine-actionable metadata through layered abstractions, LLM-driven summarization, and precise dependency tracking, enabling teams to maintain consistency, detect drift, and automate governance across entire monorepos.
responsibility: ArchSpine orchestrates a multi-layered pipeline: the CLI layer handles user commands and bootstrapping; the core engine manages L2 aggregation, validation, and state; the AST layer performs language-aware parsing and symbol extraction; the engines enforce architectural rules and detect violations; the infrastructure layer provides configuration, database persistence, LLM abstraction with retry logic, credential storage, manifest tracking, and MCP protocol exposure; the services layer coordinates check, fix, and sync pipelines with checkpoint support; the tasks layer executes staged pipeline operations (scan, AST, summarize, validate, fix, document, reverse-index, view); and the types layer defines all data contracts and configuration schemas. A companion CI/CD pipeline automates linting, testing, documentation verification, and release readiness. The system includes example projects, comprehensive test suites, and rule templates to ensure extensibility and correctness.
